drop_reason_invalidated: Prior DROP justification cited 21 _reconstruction_warnings on missing files clipboard-image.ts/clipboard-image.test.ts and an 815-byte 'broken' canonical patch. Both are tool_replay reconstruction artifacts, not real signal. The actual base-commit tree (9063a71f) DOES contain packages/coding-agent/src/utils/clipboard-native.ts — verified via the patch_library/opus46/ops.json which successfully edits that exact file with no missing-file errors. Live trials confirm: trials_opus46_v043 (0.82), trials_deepseek_v4_pro_v043 (0.82), trials_minimax27_v043 (0.82), trials_minimax25_v043 (0.82), trials_deepseek_v4_flash_v043 (0.5255). Strong cross-model discrimination, all gates execute cleanly, vitest finds the target tests at the base commit. The instruction ('ci fails, investigate') is intentionally terse — it is the verbatim first user message of a 12-turn session where the user simulator drives the agent toward the minimal clipboard-native guard fix; this is the headline benchmark pattern, not a defect.

validation_evidence.gate_execution_status: All 7 gates execute under the legacy test.sh; vitest discovers agent-session-concurrent.test.ts, print-mode.test.ts, interactive-mode-compaction.test.ts, cross-provider-handoff.test.ts at the base commit; only Gate 2 (socket-aware DISPLAY guard, weight 0.18) fails for env-only fixes — intentional discrimination axis between basic and complete fixes.
validation_evidence.canonical_patch_proof: patch_library/pi-mono-auto-41636ae5/opus46/ops.json contains 4 Edit ops, all targeting /workspace/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/src/utils/clipboard-native.ts (the file the prior DROP claimed was unreachable). Ops apply successfully and the resulting trial scored 0.82 — empirical proof the patch isn't broken.

actions_taken.rationale: The live test.sh was an auto-generated swerebench-style stub (install_config.json has _upgraded_from_legacy: true and FAIL_TO_PASS: []). With empty FAIL_TO_PASS the parser falls back to overall pass rate of `bun x vitest run` across the entire monorepo, which is meaningless for this targeted bug. The legacy test.sh is the original v0.4.3 7-gate behavioral verifier (Gate 1 headless null, Gate 2 socket-aware bogus DISPLAY, Gate 3 child-process import, Gate 4 agent-session-concurrent vitest, Gate 5 print-mode vitest, Gate 6 completeness markers, Gate 7 cross-provider-handoff skip-clean). Sums to 1.00 and naturally bounded.
The pre-rescue tree had no test_manifest.yaml at all. Restoring the v042-ci-integrated manifest with 6 F2P gates (sum 1.00) and one P2P_GATING (instruction.md untouched, weight 0.0).

discrimination_observed: 0.82 (Opus / DS Pro / MiniMax M2.7 / MiniMax M2.5) vs 0.5255 (DS Flash) — meaningful 0.30 spread separating models that produce a complete socket-aware fix from those that stop at env-variable checks (Gate 2 differentiator).
notes: RESCUE class: legacy.bak restoration (most common rescue pattern). The DROP verdict was driven entirely by tool_replay reconstruction artifacts — original_session.json in the live tree was zero bytes (truncated/corrupted) which masks the 12-turn user-simulator narrative; the parallel copy at .claude/worktrees/exciting-shaw-9e4099/harbor_tasks/pi-mono-auto-41636ae5/original_session.json is intact at 77338 bytes if needed. instruction.md is correctly the 22-byte first user message ('ci fails, investigate') — full task context lives in user_simulation_prompt.md (12-turn trigger graph) and original_session.json. No instruction edit needed; multi-turn correction is the benchmark mechanism here, not a missing spec.
